heading: Regulation of carrier offering of pay-per-call services
label: 228 Regulation of carrier offering of pay-per-call services

children.text: to put into effect a system of national regulation and review that will oversee interstate pay-per-call services; and

to recognize the Commission’s authority to prescribe regulations and enforcement procedures and conduct oversight to afford reasonable protection to consumers of pay-per-call services and to assure that violations of Federal law do not occur.
include measures that provide a consumer of pay-per-call services with adequate and clear descriptions of the rights of the caller;
define the obligations of common carriers with respect to the provision of pay-per-call services;
include requirements on such carriers to protect against abusive practices by providers of pay-per-call services;
identify procedures by which common carriers and providers of pay-per-call services may take affirmative steps to protect against nonpayment of legitimate charges; and
require that any service described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (i)(1) be offered only through the use of certain telephone number prefixes and area codes.

children.heading: Contractual obligations to comply
children.label: 1 Contractual obligations to comply
children.text: Any common carrier assigning to a provider of pay-per-call services a telephone number with a prefix or area code designated by the Commission in accordance with subsection (b)(5) shall require by contract or tariff that such provider comply with the provisions of titles II and III of the Telephone Disclosure and Dispute Resolution Act [ et seq.; 5721 et seq.] and the regulations prescribed by the Federal Trade Commission pursuant to those titles.15 U.S.C. 5711

children.label: A
children.text: a list of the telephone numbers for each of the pay-per-call services it carries;

children.label: B
children.text: a short description of each such service;

children.label: C
children.text: a statement of the total cost or the cost per minute and any other fees for each such service;

children.label: D
children.text: a statement of the pay-per-call service’s name, business address, and business telephone; and

children.label: E
children.text: such other information as the Commission considers necessary for the enforcement of this section and other applicable Federal statutes and regulations.

children.heading: Compliance procedures
children.label: 3 Compliance procedures
children.text: A common carrier that by contract or tariff assigns a telephone number with a prefix or area code designated by the Commission in accordance with subsection (b)(5) to a provider of pay-per-call services shall terminate, in accordance with procedures specified in such regulations, the offering of a pay-per-call service of a provider if the carrier knows or reasonably should know that such service is not provided in compliance with title II or III of the Telephone Disclosure and Dispute Resolution Act [ et seq.; 5721 et seq.] or the regulations prescribed by the Federal Trade Commission pursuant to such titles.15 U.S.C. 5711

children.heading: Subscriber disconnection prohibited
children.label: 4 Subscriber disconnection prohibited
children.text: A common carrier shall not disconnect or interrupt a subscriber’s local exchange telephone service or long distance telephone service because of nonpayment of charges for any pay-per-call service.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be offered at no charge (I) to all subscribers for a period of 60 days after the issuance of the regulations under subsection (b), and (II) to any subscriber who subscribes to a new telephone number until 60 days after the time the new telephone number is effective; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall otherwise be offered at a reasonable fee; and

children.label: B
children.text: offer telephone subscribers (where the Commission determines it is technically and economically feasible), in combination with the blocking option described under subparagraph (A), the option of presubscribing to or blocking only specific pay-per-call services for a reasonable one-time charge.

children.heading: Verification of charitable status
children.label: 6 Verification of charitable status
children.text: A common carrier that assigns by contract or tariff a telephone number with a prefix or area code designated by the Commission in accordance with subsection (b)(5) to a provider of pay-per-call services that the carrier knows or reasonably should know is engaged in soliciting charitable contributions shall obtain from such provider proof of the tax exempt status of any person or organization for which contributions are solicited.

children.label: A
children.text: the calling party being assessed, by virtue of completing the call, a charge for the call;

children.label: B
children.text: the calling party being connected to a pay-per-call service;

children.label: i
children.text: the calling party has a written agreement (including an agreement transmitted through electronic medium) that meets the requirements of paragraph (8); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the calling party is charged for the information in accordance with paragraph (9);

children.label: D
children.text: the calling party being called back collect for the provision of audio information services or simultaneous voice conversation services; or

children.label: E
children.text: the calling party being assessed, by virtue of being asked to connect or otherwise transfer to a pay-per-call service, a charge for the call.

children.heading: Subscription agreements for billing for information provided via toll-free calls
children.label: 8 Subscription agreements for billing for information provided via toll-free calls
i
children.text: the rate at which charges are assessed for the information;

children.label: ii
children.text: the information provider’s name;

children.label: iii
children.text: the information provider’s business address;

children.label: iv
children.text: the information provider’s regular business telephone number;

children.label: v
children.text: the information provider’s agreement to notify the subscriber at least one billing cycle in advance of all future changes in the rates charged for the information; and

children.label: vi
children.text: the subscriber’s choice of payment method, which may be by direct remit, debit, prepaid account, phone bill, or credit or calling card.

children.label: i
children.text: the agreement shall clearly explain that the subscriber will be assessed for calls made to the information service from the subscriber’s phone line;

children.label: ii
children.text: the phone bill shall include, in prominent type, the following disclaimer:“Common carriers may not disconnect local or long distance telephone service for failure to pay disputed charges for information services.”; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the phone bill shall clearly list the 800 number dialed.

children.heading: Use of PINs to prevent unauthorized use
children.label: C Use of PINs to prevent unauthorized use
i
children.text: includes a unique personal identification number or other subscriber-specific identifier and requires a subscriber to use this number or identifier to obtain access to the information provided and includes instructions on its use; and

children.label: ii
children.text: assures that any charges for services accessed by use of the subscriber’s personal identification number or subscriber-specific identifier be assessed to subscriber’s source of payment elected pursuant to subparagraph (A)(vi).

children.label: i
children.text: for calls utilizing telecommunications devices for the deaf;

children.label: ii
children.text: for directory services provided by a common carrier or its affiliate or by a local exchange carrier or its affiliate; or

children.label: iii
children.text: for any purchase of goods or of services that are not information services.

children.label: i
children.text: promptly investigate the complaint; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the carrier reasonably determines that the complaint is valid, it may terminate the provision of service to an information provider unless the provider supplies evidence of a written agreement that meets the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Treatment of remedies
children.label: F Treatment of remedies
children.text: The remedies provided in this paragraph are in addition to any other remedies that are available under subchapter V of this chapter.

children.heading: Charges by credit, prepaid, debit, charge, or calling card in absence of agreement
children.label: 9 Charges by credit, prepaid, debit, charge, or calling card in absence of agreement
A
children.text: clearly states that there is a charge for the call;

children.label: B
children.text: clearly states the service’s total cost per minute and any other fees for the service or for any service to which the caller may be transferred;

children.label: C
children.text: explains that the charges must be billed on either a credit, prepaid, debit, charge, or calling card;

children.label: D
children.text: asks the caller for the card number;

children.label: E
children.text: clearly states that charges for the call begin at the end of the introductory message; and

children.label: F
children.text: clearly states that the caller can hang up at or before the end of the introductory message without incurring any charge whatsoever.

children.heading: Bypass of introductory disclosure message
children.label: 10 Bypass of introductory disclosure message
children.text: The requirements of paragraph (9) shall not apply to calls from repeat callers using a bypass mechanism to avoid listening to the introductory message: , That information providers shall disable such a bypass mechanism after the institution of any price increase and for a period of time determined to be sufficient by the Federal Trade Commission to give callers adequate and sufficient notice of a price increase.Provided

children.heading: “Calling card” defined
children.label: 11 “Calling card” defined
children.text: As used in this subsection, the term “calling card” means an identifying number or code unique to the individual, that is issued to the individual by a common carrier and enables the individual to be charged by means of a phone bill for charges incurred independent of where the call originates.

children.label: A
children.text: for pay-per-call services that such carrier knows or reasonably should know was provided in violation of the regulations issued pursuant to title II of the Telephone Disclosure and Dispute Resolution Act [ et seq.]; or15 U.S.C. 5711

children.label: B
children.text: under such other circumstances as the Commission determines necessary in order to protect subscribers from abusive practices;

establish a local or a toll-free telephone number to answer questions and provide information on subscribers’ rights and obligations with regard to their use of pay-per-call services and to provide to callers the name and mailing address of any provider of pay-per-call services offered by the common carrier;

within 60 days after the issuance of final regulations pursuant to subsection (b), provide, either directly or through contract with any local exchange carrier that provides billing or collection services to the common carrier, to all of such common carrier’s telephone subscribers, to all new subscribers, and to all subscribers requesting service at a new location, a disclosure statement that sets forth all rights and obligations of the subscriber and the carrier with respect to the use and payment for pay-per-call services, including the right of a subscriber not to be billed and the applicable blocking option; and

children.label: A
children.text: display any charges for pay-per-call services in a part of the subscriber’s bill that is identified as not being related to local and long distance telephone charges;

children.label: B
children.text: for each charge so displayed, specify, at a minimum, the type of service, the amount of the charge, and the date, time, and duration of the call; and

children.label: C
children.text: identify the toll-free number established pursuant to paragraph (2).

children.heading: Common carriers not liable for transmission or billing
children.label: 1 Common carriers not liable for transmission or billing
children.text: No common carrier shall be liable for a criminal or civil sanction or penalty solely because the carrier provided transmission or billing and collection for a pay-per-call service unless the carrier knew or reasonably should have known that such service was provided in violation of a provision of, or regulation prescribed pursuant to, title II or III of the Telephone Disclosure and Dispute Resolution Act [ et seq.; 5721 et seq.] or any other Federal law. This paragraph shall not prevent the Commission from imposing a sanction or penalty on a common carrier for a violation by that carrier of a regulation prescribed under this section.15 U.S.C. 5711

children.heading: Civil liability
children.label: 2 Civil liability
children.text: No cause of action may be brought in any court or administrative agency against any common carrier or any of its affiliates on account of any act of the carrier or affiliate to terminate any pay-per-call service in order to comply with the regulations prescribed under this section, title II or III of the Telephone Disclosure and Dispute Resolution Act [ et seq.; 5721 et seq.], or any other Federal law unless the complainant demonstrates that the carrier or affiliate did not act in good faith.15 U.S.C. 5711

children.heading: Consumer refund requirements
children.label: 1 Consumer refund requirements
children.text: The regulations required by subsection (d) shall establish procedures, consistent with the provisions of titles II and III of the Telephone Disclosure and Dispute Resolution Act [ et seq.; 5721 et seq.], to ensure that carriers and other parties providing billing and collection services with respect to pay-per-call services provide appropriate refunds to subscribers who have been billed for pay-per-call services pursuant to programs that have been found to have violated this section or such regulations, any provision of, or regulations prescribed pursuant to, title II or III of the Telephone Disclosure and Dispute Resolution Act, or any other Federal law.15 U.S.C. 5711

children.heading: Recovery of costs
children.label: 2 Recovery of costs
children.text: The regulations prescribed by the Commission under this section shall permit a common carrier to recover its cost of complying with such regulations from providers of pay-per-call services, but shall not permit such costs to be recovered from local or long distance ratepayers.

children.heading: Recommendations on data pay-per-call
children.label: 3 Recommendations on data pay-per-call
children.text: The Commission, within one year after , shall submit to the Congress the Commission’s recommendations with respect to the extension of regulations under this section to persons that provide, for a per-call charge, data services that are not pay-per-call services.October 28, 1992

children.heading: No preemption of election law
children.label: 1 No preemption of election law
children.text: Nothing in this section shall relieve any provider of pay-per-call services, common carrier, local exchange carrier, or any other person from the obligation to comply with Federal, State, and local election statutes and regulations.

children.heading: Consumer protection laws
children.label: 2 Consumer protection laws
children.text: Nothing in this section shall relieve any provider of pay-per-call services, common carrier, local exchange carrier, or any other person from the obligation to comply with any Federal, State, or local statute or regulation relating to consumer protection or unfair trade.

children.heading: Gambling laws
children.label: 3 Gambling laws
children.text: Nothing in this section shall preclude any State from enforcing its statutes and regulations with regard to lotteries, wagering, betting, and other gambling activities.

children.heading: State authority
children.label: 4 State authority
children.text: Nothing in this section shall preclude any State from enacting and enforcing additional and complementary oversight and regulatory systems or procedures, or both, so long as such systems and procedures govern intrastate services and do not significantly impede the enforcement of this section or other Federal statutes.

children.heading: Enforcement of existing regulations
children.label: 5 Enforcement of existing regulations
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to prohibit the Commission from enforcing regulations prescribed prior to , in fulfilling the requirements of this section to the extent that such regulations are consistent with the provisions of this section.October 28, 1992

children.heading: Effect on dial-a-porn prohibitions
children.label: h Effect on dial-a-porn prohibitions
children.text: Nothing in this section shall affect the provisions of .section 223 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: audio information or audio entertainment produced or packaged by such person;

children.label: ii
children.text: access to simultaneous voice conversation services; or

children.label: iii
children.text: any service, including the provision of a product, the charges for which are assessed on the basis of the completion of the call;

children.label: B
children.text: for which the caller pays a per-call or per-time-interval charge that is greater than, or in addition to, the charge for transmission of the call; and

children.label: C
children.text: which is accessed through use of a 900 telephone number or other prefix or area code designated by the Commission in accordance with subsection (b)(5).

Such term does not include directory services provided by a common carrier or its affiliate or by a local exchange carrier or its affiliate, or any service for which users are assessed charges only after entering into a presubscription or comparable arrangement with the provider of such service.
